summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E01 真实字幕生成，重点保留创始人控制权、股权和技术债相关表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: That's a bold statement.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Richard. - We have massive tech debt.
sourceContext.nextLine: You really think that you can scale and deploy my library

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，我们现在背着很重的技术债。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: S03E01 一开场就点明团队的问题不是没功能，而是底层已经欠了一堆未来要还的账。说 massive tech debt，就是在提醒 Richard：再硬推增长，后面会被历史代码反噬。
zh-CN.usageNotes: tech debt 是程序员和产品经理都会说的词，强调为了赶进度留下的结构性欠账。比 bug 更多一层“以后会越来越贵”的意思。

sourceContext.previousLine: Rigby, we gave up our own apps so that we could bust our asses for him.
sourceContext.targetLine: Why the fuck should we give up our Pied Piper shares
sourceContext.nextLine: right when we get funded,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们凭什么要把 Pied Piper 的股份交出去？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队成员为了 Richard 放弃过自己的项目，现在却被要求再让出股权，情绪当然直接炸开。这里的 give up 不是普通放弃，而是把未来身价拱手让人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 商业语境里 give up shares / equity 很常见，通常带被迫让步的意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: All right. Uh, well, Jared, uh,
sourceContext.targetLine: we gotta, one day, figure out what we're gonna do for you, for a job.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, you'll go. You'll establish yourself, and when the time is right, you'll send for me.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们迟早得想清楚，到底该给你安排什么岗位。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Jared 对 Richard 的忠诚没变，但公司开始长大以后，连“你在组织里算什么角色”都成了现实问题。figure out 在这里带的是组织重新分工的无奈。
zh-CN.usageNotes: figure out + what/how 常用于工作里梳理方案。比 solve 更像边想边推进。

sourceContext.previousLine: He's still at Raviga for another hour. Please, go talk to him.
sourceContext.targetLine: Sorry, I can't do that. I'm already set up at Flutterbeam.
sourceContext.nextLine: Whose CEO, unprompted, said,

definitions: 已经在…那边安顿好了；已经挂靠在…
zh-CN.corePhrase: be set up at a company
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 抱歉，我不能这么做，我已经在 Flutterbeam 那边安顿好了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 被挖的人不是单纯说“我有工作了”，而是在强调自己已经被另一家公司接住了。set up at 这里隐含的是身份、资源和位置都已经落定。
zh-CN.usageNotes: be set up at somewhere 可表示已经在某处安顿、安排妥当。职场里会带一点“我已经有归属了”的感觉。

sourceContext.previousLine: So, can I keep my Hooli ID, though,
sourceContext.targetLine: so I can still come and hang out with my friends on the roof?
sourceContext.nextLine: Uh, you cannot.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这样我是不是还能回来跟朋友们在天台随便待着？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 被公司赶走的人还想保住 Hooli 屋顶社交圈，说明硅谷职场很多时候连“公司福利空间”都是身份的一部分。hang out 在这里既轻松又有点可怜。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hang out 很适合朋友间的松散社交，比 meet up 更随意，也不带正式目的。

sourceContext.previousLine: and I appreciate you being so honest with me.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, better we find out now, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yeah.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，早点现在就发现总比以后出事强，对吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型的止损话术，意思是坏消息虽然难听，但越早知道越值钱。职场里它常被用来给残酷现实包一层理性外衣。
zh-CN.usageNotes: better we find out now 是很地道的复盘句式，常用于招聘、合作、感情翻车后的自我安慰。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yes, I remember.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, you basically just loaded a gun and handed it to Hanneman,
sourceContext.nextLine: and Hanneman sold that gun to Raviga,

definitions: 给对方递刀子；等于帮他造武器
zh-CN.corePhrase: load a gun and hand it to someone
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你这基本等于把枪装好子弹再亲手递给 Hanneman。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是字面暴力，而是在说 Richard 自己把把柄和筹码都送到了 Russ 手上。创业谈判里最怕的就是无意中给对方更大的操作空间。
zh-CN.usageNotes: load a gun and hand it to someone 是很形象的责备说法，表示你亲手把危险工具交给了对手。

sourceContext.previousLine: How could they do this?
sourceContext.targetLine: How could they fire me from my own company?
sourceContext.nextLine: Just stay calm. We're gonna fight this.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他们怎么能把我从我自己的公司里踢出去？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句戳的是创始人剧里最痛的点：公司名义上是你的，控制权却未必在你手里。听懂 own company 在这里的情绪，才能懂硅谷融资的残酷。
zh-CN.usageNotes: my own company 常带强烈身份认同。和 the company 相比，更强调“这是我亲手做出来的”。

sourceContext.previousLine: How could they fire me from my own company?
sourceContext.targetLine: Just stay calm. We're gonna fight this.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesus, Richard, slow down.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 先冷静点，我们会把这事顶回去。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当公司治理反噬到创始人头上时，fight this 说的不是打架，而是去法律、董事会和程序层面反击。
zh-CN.usageNotes: fight this / fight it 常用于应对处罚、指控、决定。语气里带明显不服。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay. I'm not gonna let you do this. I'm gonna fight it.
sourceContext.targetLine: I understand you're upset.
sourceContext.nextLine: As you can see, this is a very emotional time for me as well,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我知道你现在情绪很大。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这类句子表面在安抚，实际也可能是在轻轻压低对方的发言权。董事会或 HR 说 upset，常意味着“你先别失控”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: upset 比 angry 更宽，既可指生气，也可指难过、被扰乱。

deckMetadata.title: Silicon Valley · Two in the Box

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E02 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖管理层话术、产品团队摩擦和办公室沟通表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Who knows better what the customers need
sourceContext.targetLine: than the people who actually deal with the customers every single day?
sourceContext.nextLine: We're all gonna meet at 1:00, Richard.

definitions: 直接面对客户；处理客户问题
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 难道还不如那些每天真正面对客户的人更懂吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把“懂产品的人”拉回客服和一线现场，说明公司内部已经在争谁更有资格定义用户需求。deal with customers 在这里很接地气。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal with customers 常用于零售、服务、售后场景，强调你不是拍脑袋，而是天天在前线。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hey, Jared, why don't we knock off for tonight
sourceContext.targetLine: and just pick this back up tomorrow morning.
sourceContext.nextLine: Mmm. Agreed.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这事我们明天早上再接着捡起来做吧。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 会议或冲突聊不下去时，pick this back up 是很典型的“先放一晚”的管理话术。它不等于解决了，只是暂时按下暂停键。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pick it back up 很适合会议、项目、关系恢复，表示中断后重新续上。

sourceContext.targetLine: 1 Well, Richard, according to all of your test results,
sourceContext.nextLine: you are what we in the medical field call healthy.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，根据你全部的检测结果来看，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 医生一开口用 according to all of your test results，就是在把话从主观判断切到“我有依据”。这种铺垫会让后面的结论更像正式宣告。
zh-CN.usageNotes: according to 可接 report, data, plan, rumor。比 based on 更口语一点。

sourceContext.targetLine: 1 Well, Richard, according to all of your test results,
sourceContext.nextLine: you are what we in the medical field call healthy.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，根据你所有的检查结果，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这集把 Richard 的焦虑直接拉到身体层面，test results 一出来，剧情瞬间有了医学权威感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: test results 在医疗和软件测试里都能用，要靠上下文判断是哪类测试。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Growth? - Growth.
sourceContext.targetLine: The more brilliant people we can get working here,
sourceContext.nextLine: then the faster we can get whatever is in your head out into the world.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们能招来这里工作的顶尖人才越多，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 创业公司在扩张时最爱说的不是 employees，而是 brilliant people，既在画愿景，也在给团队贴精英标签。
zh-CN.usageNotes: brilliant people 比 smart people 更夸张，常用于招聘、投资人介绍和公司自夸。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, I actually got fired as CEO of my company.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm working now as the CTO under the new CEO, Jack Barker.
sourceContext.nextLine: Wait a minute. You got a boss at your own company?

zh-CN.corePhrase: work as CTO under a CEO
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我现在是在新 CEO Jack Barker 手下做 CTO。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把组织层级说得很清楚，也暗示原本的权力版图已经彻底变了。under the new CEO 听着就有点失势后的重新站位感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: work under someone 在职场里很常见，表示向某人汇报或受其管理。

sourceContext.previousLine: I moved away from the flute-playing guy.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't know if you guys realized it, but that was a little phallic.
sourceContext.nextLine: I didn't want to waste your time with it, so I just pulled the trigger.

definitions: 很像男性生殖器的；带性暗示的
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不知道你们注意到没有，但那东西看起来有点过于性暗示了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 硅谷宅男团队做演示时常常完全不自知，这句就是美剧里经典的“你们做的东西看起来很像那玩意儿”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: phallic 是文化评论里很常见的词，常用于设计、建筑、艺术和低级玩笑。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh, Google, right? You said it at the beginning of the story.
sourceContext.targetLine: You're right. I did that wrong.
sourceContext.nextLine: And the whole point is that all of this is a sound investment

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你说得对，这件事我处理错了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这不是承认“我很糟”，而是承认某个具体操作做错了。职场里能这样说，往往是在给关系降火。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I did that wrong 比 I was wrong 更聚焦行为，听起来也更容易被接受。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hopefully, it lives up to your standards.
sourceContext.targetLine: Yeah, compared to the shit shack we're used to,
sourceContext.nextLine: anything's an improvement.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 是啊，跟我们平时待的那种破地方比起来，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句用很粗的方式吐槽团队之前的办公环境，带出创业公司常见的“我们从破车库熬出来”的叙事。
zh-CN.usageNotes: shit shack 非常口语且粗鲁，只适合吐槽熟悉环境，不适合正式场合。

sourceContext.previousLine: I moved away from the flute-playing guy.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't know if you guys realized it, but that was a little phallic.
sourceContext.nextLine: I didn't want to waste your time with it, so I just pulled the trigger.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我不知道你们有没有意识到，但它看起来真的有点过于像那个了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句值得单独学，是因为 a little phallic 的语气特别美式：不是学术评论，而是带着尴尬笑意的半委婉提醒。
zh-CN.usageNotes: a little + 负面形容词 是很地道的软化批评方式，听起来比直接说 too phallic 委婉。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E03 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖转型争议、招聘施压和平台路线分歧表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: None. That's the irony.
sourceContext.targetLine: The more meetings I turn down, the more these recruiters send me.
sourceContext.nextLine: - What are you doing? - Eating popcorn.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我越是拒绝这些会面，那些猎头就越是疯狂找我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Jared 被挖角时说 turn down meetings，说明在硅谷越稀缺的人越会被追着抢。拒绝本身反而成了价值信号。
zh-CN.usageNotes: turn down 可接 offer, meeting, invitation。比 reject 更口语也更常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: I don't know. I don't want to lie down for this, okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't want to just give up so easily.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, unfortunately, in this case, I think we have to roll over and take it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我不想就这么轻易认输。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Richard 在平台路线被改掉之后还不甘心，这句不是鸡汤，而是创始人对“我还没输够”的执念。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give up so easily 常用于鼓劲或责怪，强调“还没到该放弃的时候”。

sourceContext.previousLine: so it makes things easy.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, well, we should probably get going. Now.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Almost right now. - Yeah, right now even.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好吧，我们最好现在就赶紧走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当场面变得更尴尬或更危险时，说 get going 会比 leave 更自然，也更像要立刻撤。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get going 是非常口语的离场表达，可真是走人，也可指开始行动。

sourceContext.previousLine: but in the long term, we need to be building
sourceContext.targetLine: -our full-time platform. - Gloria!
sourceContext.nextLine: Could you call the fish guy and see what kind of warranty

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 做我们全职投入的核心平台。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 平台和盒子之争里，platform 不是某个功能，而是整个公司的真正野心。加上 full-time，表示资源和注意力都要完全砸进去。
zh-CN.usageNotes: full-time 在这里不是雇佣形式，而是强调资源投入是主线不是副业。

term.text: I don't like it any more than you do
term.lemma: not like it any more than someone does

sourceContext.previousLine: Richard, what the fuck are we doing here?
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't like it any more than you do.
sourceContext.nextLine: I told Jack we'd come check this place out and then we'd have a discussion.

definitions: 我也一样不喜欢这件事
zh-CN.corePhrase: I don't like it any more than you do
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这事我也并不比你更喜欢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句很适合上级在传坏消息时保留一点同盟感，意思是“我不是你的敌人，我只是也没办法”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: not ... any more than you do 是很实用的共情结构，常见于安抚和劝说。

sourceContext.previousLine: I told Jack we'd come check this place out and then we'd have a discussion.
sourceContext.targetLine: I can't believe you suggested the idea of building a fucking appliance.
sourceContext.nextLine: I didn't, okay?

definitions: 去做一个该死的硬件盒子
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真不敢相信你居然提议去做个该死的硬件盒子。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在纯软件理想主义者眼里，appliance 意味着笨重、低想象力、不可扩展。这里的怒气本质上是路线之争。
zh-CN.usageNotes: appliance 在科技语境里可指专用硬件设备，不只是家电。

sourceContext.previousLine: That box is artless commerce. I won't be a party to it.
sourceContext.targetLine: So, you're just gonna quit?
sourceContext.nextLine: Just like that? How can you do that?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 所以你这是打算就这么直接不干了？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 问别人 you're just gonna quit? 往往不是单纯询问，而是带着激将法和失望。
zh-CN.usageNotes: gonna quit 比 resign 生硬少很多，常见于朋友和同事之间的冲突场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: you're gonna lose all your shares.
sourceContext.targetLine: And what if Barker changes his mind and lets us build the platform?
sourceContext.nextLine: And the odds of that are?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那如果 Barker 改主意，让我们继续做平台呢？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队还在抓最后一点希望，change his mind 说的是高层一句话就能把战略翻盘。
zh-CN.usageNotes: change my/his mind 是高频日常表达，从感情到公司战略都能用。

sourceContext.previousLine: you're gonna lose all your shares.
sourceContext.targetLine: And what if Barker changes his mind and lets us build the platform?
sourceContext.nextLine: And the odds of that are?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那如果 Barker 改主意，允许我们继续做平台呢？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把团队当前地位说得很清楚: 做不做平台已经不是技术判断，而是上面让不让。
zh-CN.usageNotes: let someone do something 很基础，但在权力关系里特别关键，表示决定权不在你手里。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, we shouldn't, Richard.
sourceContext.targetLine: We're not building the platform.
sourceContext.nextLine: We're building a box.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们不做平台了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句短得像判决书。对整个团队来说，它不是一句普通否定，而是公司愿景被硬生生改轨的瞬间。
zh-CN.usageNotes: We're not doing X 常用来给讨论画句号。越短越像拍板。

deckMetadata.title: Silicon Valley · Maleant Data Systems Solutions

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E04 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖权责冲突、妥协上线和商业谈判表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: So, probably we should just push pause on the whole box thing
sourceContext.targetLine: until we figure out where to go from here.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay. I... Actually, I have a question.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 先撑到我们想明白接下来到底该往哪走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队已经走进死胡同，只能暂时把眼前项目维持住，再想下一步。这个结构很适合描述战略迷茫期。
zh-CN.usageNotes: where to go from here 是复盘和转型场景高频句，既可讲公司也可讲关系。

sourceContext.previousLine: I couldn't sit there and watch them do that to you.
sourceContext.targetLine: I just hope I don't end up fired over this.
sourceContext.nextLine: Well, what you did took incredible guts.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我只希望我别因为这事最后被开掉。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句话把小失误和饭碗直接挂钩，很符合职场高压环境里的真实恐惧。
zh-CN.usageNotes: end up + 过去分词 很实用，表示最终落到某种状态。

sourceContext.previousLine: And you are the only four people in this building that are fighting me on it!
sourceContext.targetLine: So give me one reason why I don't fire you right now,
sourceContext.nextLine: claw back your options, make you unhireable all over town.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那你现在就给我一个理由，为什么我不该立刻把你开掉。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这类上级问法不是求建议，而是逼对方马上交代价值。职场里一听就知道已经到悬崖边。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give me one reason why... 是很强势的逼问句型。后面接不 fire you / don't walk away 都很自然。

sourceContext.previousLine: That's why I wrote it.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, well, I guess I should just revert back.
sourceContext.nextLine: Right?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好吧，那我看我还是得把它回滚回去。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 工程团队做了错决定时，revert back 不是抽象反省，而是把代码或方案退回旧状态。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 技术上更常直接说 revert；口语里很多人会说 revert back，虽然略重复，但很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: and want you to kill yourself.
sourceContext.targetLine: Guys, please. Let's just shit this thing out so we can get on to building the platform.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hi.

definitions: 赶紧糊出来；先把这破东西产出来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 大家求你们了，先把这玩意儿赶紧糊出来，我们好回去做平台。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Richard 对 box 方案毫无热爱，所以用最粗鲁的方式表达“先做完交差”。这句里全是轻蔑和赶工。
zh-CN.usageNotes: shit something out 很粗，但在创意、代码、文档赶工时很常见，表示“质量先别管，先弄出来”。

sourceContext.previousLine: and want you to kill yourself.
sourceContext.targetLine: Guys, please. Let's just shit this thing out so we can get on to building the platform.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hi.

definitions: 转去做下一件更重要的事
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 先把这东西糊出来，我们就能转回去做平台了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: get on to 在这里有明显的“别再浪费时间在这上面”的急躁感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get on to something 常表示完成当前任务后继续下一项，口语里很高频。

sourceContext.previousLine: What do you want?
sourceContext.targetLine: I want to build my platform. Now.
sourceContext.nextLine: To complete the box project, it's going to take six months at least.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我现在就想去做我自己的平台。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句里的 my platform 很关键，说明 Richard 把平台当成创始人理想，而 box 只是被迫营业。
zh-CN.usageNotes: my platform / our platform 在创业语境里常带强烈的 ownership 和愿景感。

sourceContext.previousLine: All of them on the payroll, all of them here to build me a box.
sourceContext.targetLine: I have a deal, in principle, with Maleant to buy the box.
sourceContext.nextLine: The second that we deliver a product that meets those specs,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我和 Maleant 原则上已经谈妥，他们会买这个盒子。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: in principle 表示大方向谈拢了，但纸面和细节还没完全落地。对创业公司来说，这种“快成了”最容易让人上头。
zh-CN.usageNotes: in principle 常见于商业和政治语境，意思是框架同意，不代表细节签完。

sourceContext.previousLine: All of them on the payroll, all of them here to build me a box.
sourceContext.targetLine: I have a deal, in principle, with Maleant to buy the box.
sourceContext.nextLine: The second that we deliver a product that meets those specs,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Maleant 原则上已经愿意买这个盒子了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句说明原本团队鄙视的 box 方案，居然真的有市场买单。剧情张力就在理想和现金之间。
zh-CN.usageNotes: buy 在商业里不只指付款，也常表示“认可这个方案”。不过这里是字面采购。

sourceContext.previousLine: about just doing your absolute minimum of your specs,
sourceContext.targetLine: but one thing led to another and, well, Jack,
sourceContext.nextLine: buckle your seat belt because I'd like to introduce you to...

definitions: 后来事情一步步演变成这样
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 后来事情一步步发展下去，就变成了现在这样，Jack。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是很经典的轻描淡写式甩锅，说话人故意把一连串复杂失误压缩成“事情就这么发展了”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: one thing led to another 常用于解释尴尬、出轨、闯祸、项目跑偏，带回避细节的味道。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E05 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖现金压力、接班风险和公司内斗表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: and now we're dead-ass broke.
sourceContext.targetLine: Our burn rate is astronomical,
sourceContext.nextLine: and every day we sit in limbo like this...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们的烧钱速度已经夸张到离谱。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 公司一旦开始大规模运转，burn rate 就成了每天都在往下滴血的速度表。astronomical 让这句更像财务警报。
zh-CN.usageNotes: astronomical 常搭配 cost, number, burn rate，表示高得离谱。

sourceContext.previousLine: Fucking A.
sourceContext.targetLine: Look, when, or if, a new CEO comes to take over the company,
sourceContext.nextLine: we will do what they say to the best of our abilities.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 听着，等新 CEO 来接管公司的时候，如果真有那一天的话，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队已经开始认真想“创始人被换掉以后怎么办”，take over the company 的威胁在这一季越来越具体。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take over 可指接手业务，也可指夺权。语气上既能中性，也能很可怕。

sourceContext.previousLine: It jars the ear.
sourceContext.targetLine: Fine. I will have my office set up an interview.
sourceContext.nextLine: But I want you to present yourself well, Richard.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行，那我会让我的办公室安排一场面试。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句特别像高层或权力人物的说法，强调不是“我来联系”，而是我有办公室替我处理。身份感很强。
zh-CN.usageNotes: have my office set up... 是典型高位人士口吻，日常人更常说 I'll set up an interview。

sourceContext.previousLine: is to take a drill and punch a hole through it.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, calm down, Snowden. I zeroed them all out, we're fine.
sourceContext.nextLine: Welcome, welcome.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行了，冷静点，Snowden。我都把它们清零了，没事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 程序员间一旦开始拿 Snowden 开玩笑，说明对方已经被 paranoid 到像泄密者。calm down 在这里是半安抚半嘲讽。
zh-CN.usageNotes: calm down 非常高频，但对真的激动的人说有时会显得火上浇油。

sourceContext.previousLine: You need to go in to Laurie and just tell her what happened.
sourceContext.targetLine: What? It's bad enough she's going to find out what I said after this piece posts online.
sourceContext.nextLine: You want me to go in and tell her right to her face?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 什么？光是她会在这篇文章上线后知道我说了什么，就已经够糟了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在媒体场景里，find out 关乎名声和后果，不是普通“知道消息”。一旦线上稿件发出，已经没有撤回空间。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find out 后接 what/that 非常常见，尤其适合秘密曝光语境。

sourceContext.previousLine: We get our money in stages.
sourceContext.targetLine: Right? We get $2 million upfront to get us going,
sourceContext.nextLine: to design and build the product,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对吧？他们先预付给我们两百万，让我们先跑起来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 现金快断的时候，upfront money 不是 bonus，而是续命钱。听到 upfront，团队脑子里想的都是 runway。
zh-CN.usageNotes: upfront payment / pay upfront 都很常见，表示先付一部分钱。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah. There's also no CEO here to tell me not to do it, right?
sourceContext.targetLine: That's right. You're the CTO. Technically, you're the ranking officer.
sourceContext.nextLine: Fucking A.

definitions: 当前级别最高的负责人
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 没错。你是 CTO。严格说来，你现在就是级别最高的负责人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把组织权责问题推到了面前: 人不一定准备好了，但头衔会逼你接责任。
zh-CN.usageNotes: ranking officer 带明显制度感，常见于军队、警察、公司应急场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Our burn rate is astronomical,
sourceContext.targetLine: and every day we sit in limbo like this...
sourceContext.nextLine: What if we don't?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而我们就这样一天又一天地卡在半空里。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队既没彻底死，也没办法往前走，in limbo 正好形容这种无结论、无动作、无归属的状态。
zh-CN.usageNotes: in limbo 可用于签证、工作、关系、融资，全都是“悬着”。

sourceContext.previousLine: who just took $5 million in funding,
sourceContext.targetLine: and now we're dead-ass broke.
sourceContext.nextLine: Our burn rate is astronomical,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而现在我们已经穷得见底了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是财务汇报，而是带情绪的“真没钱了”。dead-ass 让 broke 的语气更像街头口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: dead-ass 在美语里可作加强语气，dead-ass broke 表示真的穷到不行。较口语。

sourceContext.previousLine: instead of the fucking Internet.
sourceContext.targetLine: Richard, the accountants finally agreed to release our books.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay.

definitions: 放出账本；开放财务账目
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，会计那边终于同意把我们的账本放出来了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: books 在这里不是书，而是公司账目。能不能 release the books，直接关系到融资、审计和内部信任。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the books 在商业英语里高频指账目。open the books / release the books 都常见。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E06 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖媒体应对、社交撒谎和工程师文化表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, none of that adds up.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, guys, check out Code/Rag.
sourceContext.nextLine: What, did the Gavin piece post?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嘿，大家快来看看这个 Code/Rag。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 工程师兴奋推荐新东西时最自然的开场就是 check out。它带的是“快来看这个很酷的玩意儿”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: check out 可以用于产品、文章、视频、现场表演，是很高频的口语邀请。

sourceContext.previousLine: Stay close.
sourceContext.targetLine: Back up, back up.
sourceContext.nextLine: You're on private property. Back to the sidewalk.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 等等，先退一下，先别往前冲。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 两连 back up 很像现场有人要失控时的即时制动。不是备份文件，而是让对方先退一步。
zh-CN.usageNotes: back up 可指后退、重说、补充证据。听语境判断。

sourceContext.previousLine: We don't know who that person is.
sourceContext.targetLine: And reporters generally don't give up their sources.
sourceContext.nextLine: Well, then I'll just have to light a fire under this reporter's ass, won't I?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 记者通常是不会把自己的消息来源供出来的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句点出新闻行业最核心的职业伦理之一。对中国学习者来说，sources 在美国媒体文化里有很重的保护意味。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give up a source 很常见于新闻、警察、情报语境，表示把线人名字交出去。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know. I know.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, make up your mind, guys,
sourceContext.nextLine: am I a Nazi or am I a guy who throws hissy fits, you know?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，你们到底能不能拿定主意啊，伙计们。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 别人来回改口时，说 make up your mind 就是嫌对方摇摆不定、浪费时间。
zh-CN.usageNotes: make up your mind 是超高频口语，从点餐到人生决定都能用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Who was that gorgeous gazelle in the driveway?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, I've said all I care to, let's move on.
sourceContext.nextLine: She let Jared have sex with her.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 该说的我都说了，我们继续下一项。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是平和推进流程，而是“我不想再聊了”的礼貌版收口。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I've said all I care to... 很适合和 let's move on 连用，语气偏强硬。

sourceContext.previousLine: but, Gleb, your last commit, you did use spaces over tabs.
sourceContext.targetLine: Um, I don't know why you'd commit to that.
sourceContext.nextLine: Uh, so, just tell me why on Earth you would do something like that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我是真不明白你为什么要把这事答应下来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: commit 在这里不是 Git 提交，而是承诺。对程序员用户来说，这个双关特别容易误判。
zh-CN.usageNotes: commit to 常接 plan, relationship, date, decision，表示正式投入或答应。

term.text: the CEO of your own company

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on, no, it's not like that.
sourceContext.targetLine: But you did tell her that you're the CEO of your own company?
sourceContext.nextLine: it came up.

zh-CN.corePhrase: the CEO of your own company
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 但你确实有跟她说，你是自己公司的 CEO 吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在约会文化里，这种头衔包装既是炫耀资本，也是社交身价。说 own company 会比 just work in tech 听起来更有戏。
zh-CN.usageNotes: of your own company 会给身份多一层“我是创始人不是打工人”的感觉。

sourceContext.previousLine: Where is this individual?
sourceContext.targetLine: Um, she's right over there.
sourceContext.nextLine: The tall dude?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嗯，她就在那边。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这种句子很适合社交怂人被朋友推着上前搭话的场景。right 会让指向更具体，也更像现场口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: right over there / right here 都是英语里高频定位说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: But I mean, honestly, how else was I going to explain that me and my friends were at a bar?
sourceContext.targetLine: That is a good point.
sourceContext.nextLine: Anytime you're near a woman, it is important to explain why.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这点你说得确实有道理。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说 good point 往往不是完全认输，而是承认对方某一枪打得准。很适合会议里部分让步。
zh-CN.usageNotes: good point 是超高频对话回应，既可真认同，也可用来先缓和再反驳。

sourceContext.previousLine: I think you'll find I'm a pretty tolerant boss,
sourceContext.targetLine: but, Gleb, your last commit, you did use spaces over tabs.
sourceContext.nextLine: Um, I don't know why you'd commit to that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不过 Gleb，你上一次提交确实用了空格而不是 tab。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是程序员圈内部梗，能瞬间暴露团队编码风格和 nerdy 争执。对外行来说是小事，对工程师来说可能像宗教战争。
zh-CN.usageNotes: spaces vs tabs 是工程师文化里的经典梗，可延伸表示“细枝末节却吵翻天”的问题。

deckMetadata.title: Silicon Valley · To Build a Better Beta

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E07 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖 beta 发布、用户验证和产品质量判断表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: All right, babe, I gotta go.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, when you check out the settings view,
sourceContext.nextLine: can you let me know if there's something weird

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嘿，你去看设置页面的时候，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 产品讨论里 check out 不只是“看一眼”，往往意味着请你带着测试心态去验证某个页面。
zh-CN.usageNotes: check out a screen / page / flow 是产品和前端沟通里很自然的说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: so not a total loss, right?
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, so I've set up a sign-up page for the beta.
sourceContext.nextLine: Now, we're gonna keep it small and secure,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好，我已经给 beta 版本搭好了一个注册页。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 一旦产品准备放人进来，sign-up page 就是“开始收用户”的仪式性入口。它听起来小，其实意味着对外开门。
zh-CN.usageNotes: sign-up page / landing page / waitlist page 是增长和产品团队常用搭配。

sourceContext.previousLine: Before we invade people's privacy, there is a conversation about ethics.
sourceContext.targetLine: Why is it that five of your users show up at this address?
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, it's buggy.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 为什么你那五个用户全都出现在这个地址上？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是在怀疑数据造假。show up at this address 的重点不是人真的来过，而是数据痕迹暴露了异常集中。
zh-CN.usageNotes: show up in data / at an address 都可表示某个记录“冒出来”。

sourceContext.previousLine: do you know how we might be able to find that bug?
sourceContext.targetLine: No, Dinesh. We are not releasing the beta.
sourceContext.nextLine: We integrated the neural net a week ago. The platform is full of bugs.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不，Dinesh。我们不会发布这个 beta。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: S03 的核心张力之一就是“要不要先放测试版”。beta 在创业剧里不只是开发阶段名词，而是速度和质量之争。
zh-CN.usageNotes: beta 既可单独用，也常说 beta release / beta users。

sourceContext.previousLine: But that is what a beta is for.
sourceContext.targetLine: You give it to people in the real world, they use it, and that is how we find the bugs.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, Reid Hoffman says

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你把产品交给真实世界的人去用，bug 就会这样被找出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型产品现实主义：你在办公室里想得再全，也不如让用户上手踩坑。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find bugs / catch bugs 都很常见。这里最值得学的是整句背后的产品逻辑。

sourceContext.previousLine: But that is what a beta is for.
sourceContext.targetLine: You give it to people in the real world, they use it, and that is how we find the bugs.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, Reid Hoffman says

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你把它交给真实世界里的用户去用，他们才会帮你把问题踩出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 工程师在办公室里容易高估自己的假设，所以 real world 在这里是对“用户现场”的致敬，也是对自嗨开发的反击。
zh-CN.usageNotes: in the real world 常拿来对比实验室、会议室或理想状态。

sourceContext.previousLine: Richard, Reid Hoffman says
sourceContext.targetLine: if you're not mortally embarrassed by the quality of your initial release,
sourceContext.nextLine: you've released too late.

definitions: 尴尬到想死；羞耻到不行
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 如果你对自己第一次发布的质量还没有尴尬到想钻地缝，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是创业圈对 MVP 精神的经典表述：首发不够粗糙，说明你发得太晚。mortally embarrassed 把这种羞耻感说得很夸张。
zh-CN.usageNotes: mortally embarrassed 是美语里很地道的夸张说法，常用于社交或公开翻车。

sourceContext.previousLine: - You sent her the beta? - I did.
sourceContext.targetLine: Are you fucking insane? It's not ready. It's riddled with bugs.
sourceContext.nextLine: Really? Cause I've been test-driving it all day, and it's pretty fucking awesome.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你疯了吗？这根本没准备好，里面全是 bug。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Richard 对 premature launch 的恐惧在这句里彻底爆开。riddled with bugs 不是零星问题，而是整个系统都像筛子。
zh-CN.usageNotes: riddled with 可接 bugs, errors, holes, inconsistencies，表示满满都是。

sourceContext.previousLine: But I can't get it to happen again, so...
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, Richard, totally out of the blue,
sourceContext.nextLine: do you know how we might be able to find that bug?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，完全是突然想到，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 对话里先说 out of the blue，往往表示后面要冒出一个突兀的问题或要求。也可能只是给自己找台阶。
zh-CN.usageNotes: out of the blue 可用于消息、电话、邀请、事故，表示毫无征兆。

sourceContext.previousLine: do you know how we might be able to find that bug?
sourceContext.targetLine: No, Dinesh. We are not releasing the beta.
sourceContext.nextLine: We integrated the neural net a week ago. The platform is full of bugs.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不，Dinesh。我们现在不发布 beta。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: beta 不是抽象概念，release the beta 是一个产品和增长都会受影响的硬动作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: release the beta / ship the beta 都常见。ship 更工程师口吻。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E08 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖公关场面、职位危机和公司形象表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: If you pushed a little harder, you could have scored right there on camera.
sourceContext.targetLine: - You should get her phone number. - Shut up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh. This is actually, uh, Jian-Yang pranking me.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你该去要她电话号码。闭嘴。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友之间起哄时，shut up 常常不是真生气，而是害羞到不想被继续架着走。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 熟人间 Shut up 可能只是打闹；正式场合用会很冲。

sourceContext.previousLine: We called this board meeting to inform you
sourceContext.targetLine: that it's time for Hooli to let go and move on without you at the wheel.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm sorry, without me? Are you firing me?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Hooli 该放手了，也该在没有你掌舵的情况下继续往前走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句像极了分手台词，却发生在公司高层权斗里。把创始人请下车时，用的是关系疗伤式语言。
zh-CN.usageNotes: let go and move on 常见于感情、失败复盘，也很适合职场里“别再纠缠过去”。

sourceContext.previousLine: We called this board meeting to inform you
sourceContext.targetLine: that it's time for Hooli to let go and move on without you at the wheel.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm sorry, without me? Are you firing me?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Hooli 应该在没有你掌舵的情况下继续前进。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 把 CEO 说成 at the wheel，是很美式的公司隐喻: 公司像车或船，谁坐驾驶位谁就掌权。
zh-CN.usageNotes: at the wheel 可字面指开车，也常比喻在负责、在掌舵。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Boy, some people. - What a creep.
sourceContext.targetLine: Sorry you had to deal with that, Jared.
sourceContext.nextLine: You and me both, friend.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jared，抱歉让你去应付刚才那一通。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这种道歉不是为小麻烦，而是为别人替你扛社交烂摊子。deal with that 里藏着体面人的愧疚。
zh-CN.usageNotes: sorry you had to deal with that 是很地道的安慰句。

sourceContext.previousLine: I heard thousands of words about rumors and the need to contain them.
sourceContext.targetLine: What do you think would happen if I didn't show up to this Vanity Fair event?
sourceContext.nextLine: I've already RSVP'd

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你觉得如果我不出席这场 Vanity Fair 活动，会发生什么？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在公关场合，show up 不只是“出现”，而是是否履行品牌和地位义务。
zh-CN.usageNotes: show up to an event / meeting 很常见。也可表示“突然冒出来”。

sourceContext.previousLine: but first, a quick glimpse with Richard Hendricks,
sourceContext.targetLine: founder and CEO of the hot, new compression platform Pied Piper,
sourceContext.nextLine: and Erlich Bachman, Chief Visionary.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这位热门新晋压缩平台 Pied Piper 的创始人兼 CEO，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 媒体介绍 Richard 时，用 compression platform 而不是 app，说明外界已经把 Pied Piper 看成底层能力公司而不是小工具。
zh-CN.usageNotes: platform 往往比 product 更大、更有故事可讲。

sourceContext.previousLine: Did you just see the skeeved-out look on Emily Chang's face?
sourceContext.targetLine: I thought she was going to walk off set.
sourceContext.nextLine: Erlich, you're lying to him,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我还以为她会直接从拍摄现场甩手走人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型媒体录制现场危机感。walk off set 说明情绪已经大到可能不顾节目流程。
zh-CN.usageNotes: walk off set / walk off stage 都表示情绪化离场，戏剧性很强。

sourceContext.previousLine: and then Google indexed it, so I've been dealing with that.
sourceContext.targetLine: Richard, Pied Piper has taken off like a rocket.
sourceContext.nextLine: More than 100,000 installs in just 10 days.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，Pied Piper 已经像火箭一样蹿起来了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 公关和媒体最爱这种夸张增长隐喻。take off 本来就有“起飞”，加 like a rocket 更是直接把增长写成神话。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take off 可用于事业、产品、歌曲、人气。加 like a rocket 会更戏剧化。

sourceContext.previousLine: And you have a last-minute invite to the Vanity Fair summit dinner tonight.
sourceContext.targetLine: Quite an honor for such a young company.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, maybe you could comment on this one?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对这么年轻的一家公司来说，这已经算很大的荣誉了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 媒体和行业人士说 young company，不是在夸你嫩，而是在强调你资历浅却已经被看见。
zh-CN.usageNotes: young company / young brand 常用于媒体报道和投资人评价。

sourceContext.previousLine: Should we take some calls?
sourceContext.targetLine: We don't do that here.
sourceContext.nextLine: I just know what I saw, Richard.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们这里不搞这一套。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 短短一句就能划清公司文化边界，意思是“别把你那套规矩带来这里”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: We don't do that here 很适合表达文化规则，简短但很有边界感。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E09 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖增长指标、用户留存和产品方向表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Besides, I think I'm getting tired of being kicked in the balls.
sourceContext.targetLine: Come on, we have a few weeks of runway left.
sourceContext.nextLine: Uh, I don't want to say exactly how much.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 拜托，我们手上还剩几周的续航时间。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 到 S03 后段，团队对 runway 的感知已经像看燃油表。再多一周都可能决定产品能不能活到下一轮。
zh-CN.usageNotes: a few weeks of runway 是创业融资语境里的常见说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay, so, I just spoke with my friend who runs a market research firm.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Mmm-hmm. - She's gonna set up a focus group
sourceContext.nextLine: for the platform, put it in the hands of some real users,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 她会安排一个焦点小组。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队终于开始认真面对“真实用户怎么想”，而不是只听工程师自己脑补。focus group 是典型市场研究动作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: focus group 常见于产品、广告、政治测试，用来收集用户反馈。

sourceContext.previousLine: I can assure you that there is no Skynet type of situation here.
sourceContext.targetLine: No. Pied Piper will in no way become sentient and try to take over the world.
sourceContext.nextLine: He just told us he couldn't predict it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不会的。Pied Piper 绝对不会觉醒然后试图统治世界。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句用 AI 科幻梗来消解技术焦虑，既搞笑也体现工程师对“系统会不会失控”的底层想象。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take over the world 常用于夸张玩笑，也可真用于反派叙事。

sourceContext.previousLine: I can assure you that there is no Skynet type of situation here.
sourceContext.targetLine: No. Pied Piper will in no way become sentient and try to take over the world.
sourceContext.nextLine: He just told us he couldn't predict it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Pied Piper 绝不会突然变得有自我意识，然后去统治世界。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: sentient 这个词看似偏科幻，但现在在 AI 语境里很常见。这里的笑点就是把产品 bug 夸张成机器觉醒。
zh-CN.usageNotes: sentient 常见于科幻、AI 讨论和哲学问题，比 conscious 更偏“具备感知和意识”。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know, I've been showing people the video-chat app
sourceContext.targetLine: -that I hacked together and... - Shut up, Dinesh!
sourceContext.nextLine: You ungrateful pricks, all of you.

definitions: 临时拼出来；草草写出来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那是我临时拼出来的，然后……闭嘴，Dinesh！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 工程师会用 hacked together 自嘲某个临时方案很粗糙但能跑。听起来轻松，实则在暗示技术债。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hack together 常用于原型、脚本、临时演示。语气比 build 更像“先凑一个”。

sourceContext.previousLine: but at least it's something.
sourceContext.targetLine: Don't give up. Richard, come on, not yet.
sourceContext.nextLine: We've got to hang on, we've got to see what happens.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 别放弃，Richard，拜托，现在还不是认输的时候。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是团队快散架时最直接的打气。not yet 很关键，表示情况糟，但还没到终局。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Don't give up 常见，但搭配 not yet 会更有“还没到时候”的味道。

sourceContext.previousLine: installs isn't really the metric that matters.
sourceContext.targetLine: We need daily active users, people who come back to the platform.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay, so how many daily users do you actually have?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们需要的是日活用户，也就是会每天回到平台的人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这集标题就是 Daily Active Users，说明团队终于开始从“能不能做出来”转向“用户会不会回来”。这是创业思维的一次硬切换。
zh-CN.usageNotes: daily active users 常缩写 DAU，是产品和增长岗位最核心的指标之一。

sourceContext.previousLine: installs isn't really the metric that matters.
sourceContext.targetLine: We need daily active users, people who come back to the platform.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay, so how many daily users do you actually have?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们需要的是那些会一次次回到平台的人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 比下载量更值钱的是回访。come back to the platform 讲的是留存，不是拉新。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come back to 口语很基础，但在增长语境里常和 retention 一起理解。

sourceContext.previousLine: and we spent a fucking bazillion dollars, on the tables ad
sourceContext.targetLine: and now we are almost at 500,000 installs, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: - Yeah. What's the problem? - Well, as you know,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而我们现在已经快到五十万次安装了，对吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句提醒观众，下载量会很好看，但未必等于产品健康。正因为有 installs，才更需要对照 DAU。
zh-CN.usageNotes: install 作名词在产品分析里很常见。注意和 installation 区分口气，前者更产品化。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay, so, I just spoke with my friend who runs a market research firm.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Mmm-hmm. - She's gonna set up a focus group
sourceContext.nextLine: for the platform, put it in the hands of some real users,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 她会去安排一个焦点小组。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 对技术团队来说，focus group 有时像外行来评头论足；但在增长阶段，它又常常是不得不补的一课。
zh-CN.usageNotes: focus group 可单独当名词使用，和 survey、user interview 同属研究方法。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S03E10 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖融资尽调、股权结构和增长异动表达.

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, I suppose.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, especially during due diligence,
sourceContext.nextLine: like the kind done by VCs or possible future reviews

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，尤其是在尽调阶段，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 到了融资尾声，due diligence 就像最后一道照妖镜。故事、账本、代码、用户数据都会被拿出来过筛。
zh-CN.usageNotes: during due diligence 很适合描述融资进入深水区。这个词在并购和投资里极高频。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, the, uh...
sourceContext.targetLine: This is an actual term sheet. And once I sign this... That's it, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yup. No turning back.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这可是一份真的 term sheet。我一旦签了，就算定了，对吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Richard 终于拿到实打实的条款书，语气里既兴奋又害怕。actual 很关键，说明这次不是空谈，而是真要承担后果。
zh-CN.usageNotes: actual 在这里是“不是嘴上说说”。term sheet 一旦签字，通常就进入强约束谈判。

sourceContext.previousLine: Richard, sign it.
sourceContext.targetLine: That's two years of runway.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Sign it now! - I can't.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那可就是整整两年的续航。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 对一个总在为现金发愁的创业团队来说，两年 runway 听起来几乎像赦免令。
zh-CN.usageNotes: X years of runway 是很标准的融资表达，越长越意味着喘息空间。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, when a tech company reboots, it's normal to have a conversation
sourceContext.targetLine: about restructuring the cap table.
sourceContext.nextLine: Good, 'cause I'm the guy that hacked together the video-chat app

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 关于重整股权结构表的事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 融资到后期，真正复杂的往往不是产品，而是 cap table。它决定每个人到底还剩多少利益和控制权。
zh-CN.usageNotes: cap table 是 startup 圈硬词，指 capitalization table。常和 dilution, equity 一起出现。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know?
sourceContext.targetLine: Two days ago, I wanted to look at user behavior to figure out
sourceContext.nextLine: what spurred the sudden uptick, and well, I noticed something.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 两天前我想看看用户行为，到底想搞清楚什么在发生。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 数据突然异动时，团队第一反应不是庆祝，而是 figure out 到底发生了什么。这就是产品人看增长的本能。
zh-CN.usageNotes: figure out 常跟 data, behavior, pattern 搭配，表示从信息里理出原因。

sourceContext.previousLine: or he would get very Italian on me.
sourceContext.targetLine: And I'm not interested to find out what that means.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Okay. Shall we? - To Coleman Blair! Let's close this fucker.

definitions: 去亲自知道那到底意味着什么
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而我一点也不想亲自去知道那到底意味着什么。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型的“我已经能猜到后果很糟，所以我不想验证”的逃避式反应。
zh-CN.usageNotes: not interested to find out... 语气里常带怕麻烦、怕真相或不想卷进去。

sourceContext.previousLine: Take it out in pieces.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't mean to interrupt, but I could call in a personal favor
sourceContext.nextLine: and get a helo crew out of Travis AFB with a Sikorsky Skycrane to airlift it out

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我不是想打断，但我可以动用一个私人情面。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 硅谷做事经常不只是拼制度，也拼谁能在关键时刻 call in a favor。这里听起来就很像“我有一张隐藏牌”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: call in a favor 表示去动用以前欠下或建立的人情。是很地道的社交资源表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Two days ago, I wanted to look at user behavior to figure out
sourceContext.targetLine: what spurred the sudden uptick, and well, I noticed something.
sourceContext.nextLine: Almost all of our new users were just uploading a single photo

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 是什么引发了这波突然的增长，然后我注意到了一件事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: uptick 比 increase 更像数据面板上突然抬头的一截曲线。这一集的 suspense 就建立在它看起来太不正常。
zh-CN.usageNotes: uptick 常用于 data, sales, traffic, infections，表示明显的小幅上扬。

sourceContext.previousLine: Two days ago, I wanted to look at user behavior to figure out
sourceContext.targetLine: what spurred the sudden uptick, and well, I noticed something.
sourceContext.nextLine: Almost all of our new users were just uploading a single photo

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 到底是什么刺激出了这波突然上涨，我后来注意到了一件事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: spur 让整个分析听起来更像调查原因，而不是随便猜。产品和商业分析里很爱用这个词。
zh-CN.usageNotes: spur growth / spur demand / spur an uptick 都很常见，语气比 cause 更正式。

sourceContext.previousLine: - You're not funny? - No, Gilfoyle, but that is.
sourceContext.targetLine: No, it's that everybody thinks Pied Piper is in this death spiral,
sourceContext.nextLine: so it would be inappropriate to laugh at my hysterical joke.

definitions: 死亡螺旋；越坏越坏的下坠循环
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不是，是所有人都以为 Pied Piper 已经掉进死亡螺旋了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 创业公司一旦被市场贴上 death spiral 标签，外界会默认它越跌越快、根本翻不回来了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: death spiral 常见于公司、舆论、价格系统。带非常强的下坠感。
